    1. ‌Audits And Internal Reviews

      Internal reviews will be completed periodically, and at the latest annually, a review of the Heavy Vehicle Safety Management System and supporting forms/records is to be completed or as and when nominated by the Director. The data collated from any audits or internal reviews conducted will be provided to the Director, and any non-conformance raised will be communicated, including the follow up of any corrective action that has been implemented. The Director will nominate a suitable competent person to conduct the system review at least annually, and a formal response on the findings is to be communicated.


      Any external person appointed to conduct the internal review shall have applicable qualifications and relative industry experience as noted below:



      Qualifications


      Essential


      Additional experience

      Min 5 years’ systems management experience in Quality systems


      YES

      Qualified systems auditor or similar to ISO9001 standards

      YES


      Min 5 years’ transport/logistics management experience


      YES

      Management Systems Auditor/Lead Auditor

      YES



      A formal report will be requested, created and documented, and this shall be the reference point when conducting all future planned audits or review focus points. The Director will further investigate areas of continuous non-conformity or where corrective actions have not been effective enough to eliminate any re- occurrence, and suitable actions are to be allocated and reviewed for effectiveness. An up to date CoR or systems audit tool is to be used on each occasion as the review is conducted, and reference to previously completed reviews will be part of the process of measurement of performance.


      Subject to the outcome of any conducted internal review or audit, the process shall involve formally recording the findings and subjected outcomes. Any advised action shall be controlled and monitored through the required process until a preventative or corrective action is achieved. A record of any non-conformances to be raised as a result shall be formally recorded within the system. Consultation with the Director and other senior staff may be required, and their involvement will be to provide direction for change.


  9. ‌Improvement

    [INSERT COMPANY NAME] recognises that there will always be opportunity to make improvements in how we do business or operate. There are many sources of information which will help us to do this and ensuring we capture and review this information is important.


        1. ‌Scope

          This procedure describes what action to take if non-conformances in processes or work completed is detected during any activity within the daily activities of [INSERT COMPANY NAME]. This applies to all work carried out by [INSERT COMPANY NAME], both on-site and off-site, when completing works as directed. For quality non- conformances, this procedure is applied to determine if the root cause was directly related to a system fault or human error, which may be caused by non-compliance with a procedure and or process.


          For safety non-conformances, this procedure is applied to eliminate any safety hazards, unsafe work practices and unsafe site conditions, quarantine non-compliant products and consult with personnel about changes made to address safety non-conformances.


          For quality, safety and environmental non-conformances, this procedure is applied again to determine where the root cause commenced and apply the applicable cleanup measures to control or reduce the risk. If a clean-up is required, the restoration of polluted areas and rectification methods suitable to control the initial

          risk from escalating shall be recorded. All records of non-conformances are to be recorded on the Non- Conformance, and this record will remain a live document until such time as the detail and closeout have all been completed and recorded in the register.


        2. ‌Responsibilities

          The Director is responsible for the following, with the assistance of administrative staff:

          • Evaluating non-conformances and deciding how to rectify them (with the affected parties e.g. our service, customer where required).

          • Verifying that rectification has been successfully implemented from the controls applied

          • Maintaining the non-conformance reporting system and the assessment of all reported NCR’s


        3. ‌Communication Of Non-Conformances And Corrective Actions

    Where [INSERT COMPANY NAME] takes corrective action to resolve a non-conformance of any kind it is recorded on the Non-conformance Register and a Non-conformance Report is completed. Where appropriate, or requested to do so, the Director will forward copies of applicable Non-conformance reports to all parties concerned or affected to ensure complete awareness of resolution methods or requirements.


      1. ‌Incident and Non-Conformance Reporting and Investigation

        To ensure we learn from incidents and near misses and non-conformances, all incidents, near misses and non- conformances must be reported in the SHEQ System We take a risk-based approach to investigation and where there was either a high severity or high potential Severity, we will use detailed investigation techniques to ensure we develop robust improvement actions. Where the outcomes were lower potential, basic investigation methods are used, and actions developed to prevent re-occurrence.


        The Incident Management Plan describes the processes to be followed if an incident occurs. This includes investigating, responding to, taking action to minimise any harm caused by incidents and taking action to prevent incidents from re-occurring. Incidents include injuries, environmental spills, damage to plant and equipment, near misses and any other dangerous occurrence.


        Our SHEQ System will be used to capture the details of the incident investigations and supports us to track the actions that we have developed to ensure that they are closed out. This system also allows good data capture and supports us to analyse performance and look for trends in an efficient way.


        Related Documents

        Incident Management Procedure

        SHEQ-PRO-011


      2. ‌Corrective Actions

        There are many sources of corrective actions across the business. Having a robust process for tracking improvement actions ensures that they are acted on in a timely way and that the improvements are effective.


        All SHEQ related corrective actions shall be recorded in the SHEQ System to facilitate visible monitoring and tracking of compliance by management.


        Related Documents

        Incident Management Procedure

        SHEQ-PRO-011

      3. ‌Continuous Improvement

        [INSERT COMPANY NAME] aims to focus on continually improving across all areas of our business. We do this through having processes which support us to embed learning and improvement.


        Our improvement objectives are informed by our investigation of incidents and non-conformances, workplace inspections and audits, examination of risk controls, customer feedback analysis, project reviews and through knowledge sharing across our projects.


        The Management review process is used to drive and embed improvement planning through establishing what needs to be improved and HOW that will be done.


        Related Documents

        Corrective Actions and Non-Conformance Procedure

        SHEQ-PRO-006

        Management Review Agenda

        SHEQ-FRM-015


        1. ‌NHVAS & Notifiable Occurrence Reporting

          What is a Notifiable Occurrence?

          A significant or major accident or incident that involves an NHVAS nominated vehicle and or driver operating under an Advance Fatigue Management (AFM) or Basic Fatigue Management (BFM) accreditation that has, or could have, caused significant property damage, serious injury or death and may include any of the following:

          • An accident or incident involving an NHVAS-nominated vehicle or driver in which a person is injured or killed

          • An accident or incident involving an NHVAS-nominated vehicle, or a driver operating under an AFM or BFM

          • A fire in an NHVAS-nominated vehicle

          • A medical emergency involving the driver of an NHVAS-nominated vehicle or a driver operating under an AFM or BFM accreditation.


            An injury is defined as requiring hospitalisation. Workplace slips, trips & falls are not notifiable occurrences.

          • A significant accident or incident involves injuries requiring hospitalisation and/or damage to property which presents a significant risk that requires considerable effort to avoid escalation of the impact during recovery.

          • A major accident or incident involves a fatality and/or damage to property with a major impact on life, property or the environment generally accompanied by high-level emergency response and clean-up and recovery operation.


            Timeframes for Lodgement

            Notifiable occurrences must be lodged on the NHVR Portal under the Accreditation section and within specific time frames. It is the responsibility of the Fleet & Assett Manager (NHVAS Manager) to report to the NHVR.


            Incident Category

            Submitted

            Significant – Involving dangerous goods

            Within 48 hours

            Significant – any other

            Within 7 days

            Major – involving dangerous goods

            Within 24 hours

            Major – any other

            Within 48 hours

        2. ‌Incident Investigation & Analysis

          The results of incident investigations are to be made available to the relevant people and reported under internal reporting. A copy of the Incident Notification Report (once completed) is to be forwarded to the Scheduler/ Allocator for analysis and, if necessary, further action or instruction.


          The Scheduler/ Allocator will analyse relevant information relating to incidents and determine whether any improvements or amendments can be made to the [INSERT COMPANY NAME] procedures or related documents in the prevention of any incident reoccurrence. In particular, attention will be directed towards identifying the development of trends in incidents.


          A review of reported incidents that may or have had an effect on the environment is to be reviewed and assessed under the Management Review process.


        3. ‌Motor Vehicle Accident Response Procedure

          In the event of an accident:

          • Stop the vehicle and render assistance, if able to do so

          • Call emergency services on 000, where required

          • Park the vehicle in a safe location, with hazard lights on

          • Call the Scheduler/ Allocator to advise of accident and obtain further instructions

          • Take photos of the scene

          • Take details of any other person(s) and vehicle(s) involved in the accident

            • Obtain the facts: Witnesses, a sketch of scene, relevant conditions of weather/light etc.

            • Name and address of the driver of other vehicle

            • Make type and registration number of other vehicle

            • Name and address of owner of other vehicle

            • Name, address and telephone number of any witnesses

            • Name and number of any police officer present

            • Sketch of the accident scene, showing all relevant details.

          • Trace sequence of events: identify and record all contributing unsafe conditions/acts

          • Complete Paperwork

          • Do not admit liability or sign any statements before consultation with management


    If a police officer is not present, it is the workers' responsibility to report details of the accident to the nearest police station if required by the appropriate State laws.


    In every instance of an accident on a public road, where a vehicle is immobile and blocks or partly blocks the highway, the police must be notified immediately. You must assist the police but do not answer any questions or make any statements that amount to an admission of liability for the accident or that may involve [INSERT COMPANY NAME] in any liability.

    Workers must NOT, under any circumstances, abandon an immobilised vehicle in a remote or otherwise unsafe area where the vehicle and its load may be subject to theft.


    NB: Under no circumstances can anyone be carried in any vehicle unless they are correctly seated within the cabin and appropriately restrained by a seat belt.


  10. ‌Metrics

    CoR metrics are a key part of the continual monitoring and review process. They are assigned to organisational objectives and allow performance to be measured, enable leadership to evaluate progress, assist in decision making and enhance accountability. HVCoR metrics are compiled by the NHVAS Record Keeper and reported as per monthly NHVAS Compliance Reviews. The metrics and their data sources used by [INSERT COMPANY NAME] are tabulated below:


    CoR focus area

    Hazard and incident reports Telematics System

    National Driver Work Diary

    working hours exceed the prescribed limits

    Speeding

    Surveillance reports

    Telematics System

    Speeding

    Service Records

    Mass management

    Weighbridge dockets

    Dimensions

    Daily Load Sheets

    Load restraint

    Pre-start checklists

    Maintenance

    Services required


    [INSERT COMPANY NAME]

    Docket Management System

    Maintenance

    [INSERT COMPANY NAME] Docket

    Management System

    Maintenance

    Notice Register


  11. ‌Amendment register

    AMENDMENT RECORD

    Reason for

  12. ‌Appendix A – Drug & Alcohol Procedure


    SHEQ-PRO-018

    Drug and Alcohol Procedure


    Version No.

    Issue Date

    Details

    Prepared & Approved By

    1

    DRAFT

    DRAFT

    Head of HR and Recruitment

    2

    Issued

    Final

    CEO


    3


    Reviewed


    Final review with changes

    Head of HR and Recruitment

    and Cheif Operating Officer, SHEQ











    1. ‌Purpose

      The purpose of this procedure is to support [INSERT COMPANY NAME] commitment to maintain a safe and efficient work environment through drug and alcohol testing.


    2. ‌Scope

      This procedure applies to all workers who carry out work in any capacity for [INSERT COMPANY NAME], including an employee, contractor, subcontractor, labour hire, trainee or volunteer.

    3. ‌Responsibility

      Title / Position

      Responsibility

      Chief Executive Officer and Chief Operating Officer

      Cheif Operating Officer, Safety, Health, Environment and Quality

      Workers (Employees, Contractor, Subcontractor, Labour Hire, Trainee and/or Volunteer)

      Managers/Supervisors

      • Promote the procedure and applicable policy across Eire Construction

      • Promote and implement the procedure and policy across Eire Construction.

      • Review the relevant policy and procedure to ensure best practice.

      • Provide advice and support to workers on the management system to assist Eire in creating a safe working environment.

      • Engage the testing provider under the procurement policy

      • Adhere to and maintain knowledge of this procedure.

      • Present to work in a fit for work state.

      • Attend training and education sessions as required.

      • Report any issues that may affect their fitness to work to their manager.

      • Be available to participate in testing procedures.

      • Cooperation in the process of determining fitness for work while using prescription or pharmacy medication in consultation with a treating medical practitioner.

      • Ensure workers comply with all requirements of this procedure and the responsibilities contained within the relevant health and safety duty statement.

      • Ensure all workers are trained and educated in and have access to this procedure.

      • Ensure corrective actions are implemented to manage the risks associated with fitness for work issues of workers under their supervision.

      • Assist in organising random testing as required.

      • Monitor and report any issues that may affect workers fitness for work.

      • Request post-incident or suspicion testing.

      • Attend positive and over-the-limit test interviews, if requested, as part of the discipline process.

      • Ensure this procedure is implemented and applied consistently in their operational area.

      • Treat all information collected with the strictest confidentiality.

    4. ‌Definitions

      Word/Acronym

      Meaning

      Fit for Work

      A state (physically, mentally and emotionally) to perform assigned tasks competently and in a manner which does not compromise or threaten the health, wellbeing and safety of themselves or others in the workplace.

      Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

      A confidential, professional counselling service available to all workers.

      Negative (Drug Test Result)

      The result from the first test of a sample that indicates no presence of substance being tested for.

      Non-negative Test


      The result from the first test of a sample that may indicate the presence of a substance being tested for.

      Blanket Testing

      A process by which every employee is tested on a site, location or work group.

      Targeted Testing

      A process where employees within the following scenarios will be tested: reasonable suspicion, an incident occurring and/or a positive drug test result previously.

      Over the Limit (Alcohol Test Result)

      The result of a breath test sample, which indicates a BAC more than the limit.

      Under the Limit (Alcohol Test Result)

      The result from the test of a breath sample which indicates a BAC less than the limit.

      Positive (Drug Test Result)

      The result from a laboratory confirmation that the presence of a substance being tested exceeds the limit specified in the relevant Australian Standard.

      Random

      Having no specific pattern, purpose or objective

      Reasonable suspicion

      An objectively justifiable suspicion based on facts or circumstances that justifies a request for testing at the time. The facts or circumstances are not necessarily medically based, but formed through events and observations that are general indicators of being under the influence of a drug and/or alcohol.

      Testing Provider

      [INSERT COMPANY NAME] testing provider as selected through the SHEQ team

    5. ‌Procedure


Awareness and Training

[INSERT COMPANY NAME] recognises that it is important to develop a workplace culture through awareness and training where employees are encouraged to be safe and fit for work.

All advertisements for potential employees will inform potential employees that a medical assessment will be undertaken, including a drug and alcohol prior to employment commencing and that an ongoing drug and alcohol policy is in place during employment screening.

The induction process will include a Safety Induction which includes an overview of the Drug and Alcohol Policy.


Pre-employment Medical (including Drug and Alcohol testing)

All potential employees will complete a Medical Assessment (including Drug and Alcohol testing) prior to an offer of employment being extended. Potential employees who do not successfully complete the Medical will not be eligible for an offer of employment.


Random testing

Random drug and alcohol testing may be conducted for all workers.

Random selection will be based on sites, not individuals, given the geographic nature of our workplaces.

All workers of the random site will be tested by the provider or a trained employee tester for drug and alcohol testing.


Reasonable suspicion

All workers have an obligation to submit a case for reasonable suspicion testing if they suspect that another worker is unfit for work due to drugs and/or alcohol. It is the workers right to protect the health and safety of themselves and others. A member of the Senior Leadership team will determine if the test will be completed or not based on the case received by the worker.


Post incident testing

A drug or alcohol test may be conducted in accordance with this procedure for any worker who is involved in or contributed to an incident which has resulted in:

  1. Death or serious injury of any person.

  2. Significant damage to property.

  3. Damage or incident involving plant or a motor vehicle.

  4. The potential to cause death, significant damage, or serious injury to any person.


Testing will be conducted as soon as possible following the incident. The health and safety of the worker must take precedence over the drug and alcohol testing.


Testing methods

Testing will be outsourced to a qualified testing company who will administer urine specimen testing at preemployment stage.


During the employment period, testing will be conducted by an alcoholiser and oral swabs in accordance with the relevant Australian Standards (AS/NZS 4760:2019) by a trained employee of [INSERT COMPANY NAME].


Authorised Drug and Alcohol Testing

The Cheif Operating Officer SHEQ is authorised to engage the testing provider. The testing provider will be responsible for the testing equipment and any requirements for calibration and maintenance.


Drug and Alcohol testing

Workers will be considered unfit for work if the sample collected and analysed in accordance with the guidelines set out in this procedure indicates the presence of a substance at or above the limits, or the presence of alcohol.


Testing results

All testing results will be treated in the strictest of confidence.


Testing negative

If the sample tested records a negative result, the worker is fit for work and can return to normal duties.


If the worker declares the use of prescription or pharmacy medications prior to presenting for testing and tests non-negative for a substance known to be in that medication, the worker will be considered fit to work, and allowed to continue normal duties if the medication does not cause impairment, risk of impairment, or place them outside the legal limits for operating plant and equipment (including motor vehicles). An independent Medical Advisor and further testing may be undertaken as part of this process to ensure the risk of impairment is managed in accordance with the position the worker performs.


Testing positive / non-negative with no medication disclosed

Where a worker tests positive, the worker will be stood down and transported home. The worker will be immediately stood down (Eire paid suspension) until a confirmation test is received by a third-party provider arranged by the SHEQ team.


The test results will be provided to the HR team for review. If a worker is found to have to have confirmed drug and alcohol in the third-party tests, the employee will be required to take leave out of their accrued balance. HR will support this conversation.


The workers will be requested by HR for an explanation of this breach, and this will be considered during the investigation. An investigation into this breach will be conducted and may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination of employment. As part of this review, HR will conduct a file review for any other discipline awarded to the employee.


If an investigation does not result in a termination, then one negative alcohol and/or urine drug tests (instant urine test) will be required (at the workers expense) before an employee may recommence work.


Should a negative test not be provided within a reasonable timeframe (generally within a week) then further disciplinary process may be taken against the employee.


The worker is not to return to work until a negative sample is provided.


When the employee returns to work after receiving a non-negative, a further maintenance test (instant urine test) one week following their return to work is required at their own expense.


An employee that tests positive for the first and second occasion may be supported through EAP and any other appropriate referrals to support rehabilitation.


A return to work may be conditional on completion of a rehabilitation program and/or participation in further testing.


Where an employee breaches this procedure/policy, they will be investigated and may be issued a written warning upon each occasion. Where other disciplinary outcomes have been awarded against a worker previously (or repeat breaches of this procedure), these will be considered holistically in determining whether further disciplinary action leading up to termination of employment is warranted.


If the worker is a Contractor or Subcontractor, then the SHEQ team will contact the relevant employer before arranging transport home where able. Appropriate follow-up will need to be determined with the recommendation that this contractor/subcontractor is no longer used.


Refusal to test

Where a worker refuses to be tested, or is found to tamper or evade a test, it will be considered a serious breach of this procedure.


If the worker refuses this procedure, it will count as a positive result, and they will immediately be suspended and required to Show Cause why their employment should not be terminated.


The worker is unable to attend the workplace until a negative test result is received.


Reporting of Results


The SHEQ team member working with the testing agency or conducting the testing will contact only the GM SHEQ if a non-negative test is returned.


The GM SHEQ will then contact the Chief Operations Officer and the Head of HR.


This incident/breach will be recorded in the Internal System and marked confidential, so access is restricted. All test results will be sorted in a limited access folder in the HR system.

  1. ‌Employee acknowledgement


    I, (Please print name)

    have been issued with a copy and training in relation to the content of the [INSERT COMPANY NAME] Heavy Vehicle Safety Management System which has been explained.


    • I understand the responsibilities and duties of my position and engagement and will abide by the rules, conditions, laws, regulations instruction and procedures advised now and into the future.


    • I will not disclose or discuss the contents of this Heavy Vehicle Safety Management System with other persons other than those engaged by [INSERT COMPANY NAME]


    • I acknowledge that this Heavy Vehicle Safety Management System remains the property of [INSERT COMPANY NAME] and is to be returned to the company in the event of my engagement being terminated or any separation of the employment of the company for whatever circumstance or reason.


    • I agree to conform to the requirements as mentioned in the Heavy Vehicle Safety Management System and provide records, evidence and information on an ongoing basis.


    • I acknowledge that I have read and understand the content of this Heavy Vehicle Safety Management System and I agree to conditions as explained in detail.
